weather_line: Deep fog in the hollow · something drips pleasantly · the arch holds

stories.headline: Below the Arch: What the Hollow Keeps
stories.byline: By our Highland Field Correspondent, dispatching from a mossy rock
body: Day three on Hollow Crown Ridge, and Christine and Steve have descended into the hollow below the unmapped arch — a steep, root-laced scramble that the Jeep watched from above with the stoic dignity of a parent at a school play. What they found at the bottom, according to multiple witnesses (Steve, a confused grouse, and one very damp fern), was a second arch.

Smaller than the first, half-swallowed by the ridgeline, and entirely unlisted on any Ministry Cartography Office chart, the lower arch frames a single shaft of morning light that strikes a flat stone shelf at precisely ten past eight — long enough to read by, short enough to feel chosen. Christine stood in the light. Steve took a photograph. The grouse was unmoved.
Ministry surveyors, contacted for comment, confirmed they have 'no record of a second arch' and requested that Christine please stop finding things they've missed. She has not committed to this.

stories.headline: Vault 793 Dispute Resolved Before the Kettle Boiled; Goblins File Complaint About the Speed
body: In a development that has unsettled the Gringotts dispute-resolution office, Christine cleared the Vault 793 sub-ledger backlog — a matter that has festered since the Goblin Accord of 1987 — in the time it took Griphook the Younger to locate his reading spectacles.

Senior goblins are said to be 'professionally offended.' One filed a formal grievance noting that a resolution of this magnitude 'should have taken at minimum a fortnight and several accusatory owls.' Christine's four-word summary of the counter-grievance ('that column was wrong') has been framed and hung in the staff corridor, which no one will admit to doing.

stories.headline: Interim Commander Lucy Issues Statement on Her Own Whereabouts; Statement Contains No Information
body: With Christine still afield, Lucy — appointed Interim Commander of household operations on Thursday — has issued her first formal communication: a single sheet of parchment, blank except for a paw print and the word 'fine.' When pressed for specifics regarding the missing half of Jersey's good blanket, Lucy gestured toward Ellie.

Ellie, when contacted, was in the kitchen asking questions of the toaster. She confirmed she had no knowledge of the blanket, and then added that she had also met a very friendly toaster. Jersey issued no comment from the sofa, where he has been governing in repose since Tuesday and sees no reason to change approach.

stories.headline: Level Six Vending Machine Quietly Retires; Leaves Note Saying 'My Work Here Is Done'
stories.byline: By our Ministry of Magic correspondent
body: In a brief and dignified ceremony attended by two maintenance wizards and a confused intern, the Level Six vending machine was decommissioned Friday morning, found to be 'operating outside its remit' following weeks of unsolicited dispensing. A handwritten note taped to its coin slot read: 'Everything went to the right person. Good luck.' The note was signed with a small drawing of a briefcase.

The Ministry has declined to comment on whether the machine was ever sentient. The replacement unit arrived this afternoon and has so far dispensed only crisps, which officials describe as 'a relief.'

familiars_corner: Lucy: Released a brief follow-up statement clarifying that the missing blanket corner 'was already like that,' which is both plausible and completely unprovable — a masterwork of household law.
Ellie: Spent the morning in warm conversation with her reflection in the oven door, and reports that she has made an excellent new friend who is 'very agreeable and has the same face.'

Jersey: Has colonised the warm patch of floor recently vacated by the vending-machine repairman's delivery crate, which he considers a perfectly logical expansion of his territory, and has gone back to sleep.
founding_editor_note: That second arch wouldn't have stayed hidden if the hollow had been properly surveyed in the first place — you need a transit level and someone willing to get muddy. I could have found it in an afternoon. Proud of the girl. — D.

in_brief.sport: The Holyhead Harpies confirmed their new Seeker can lift the team bus; scouts note this is 'not technically a requirement but we respect it.'
in_brief.forecast: Mist will linger in the hollow until midmorning, then the light will come through the arch at exactly the right angle, as it apparently always does — the ridge does not make mistakes.

storyline_updates.title: The Hollow Crown Expedition
storyline_updates.summary: Christine and Steve descended into the hollow below the first arch and discovered a second, smaller, entirely unmapped arch that frames a shaft of morning light at ten past eight. Ministry cartographers are embarrassed. The flat stone shelf at the bottom appears significant — smooth, central, and deliberately flat in a way that feels less geological and more purposeful. Tomorrow: the final day on the ridge; what the shelf is for, and whether the hollow yields one last thing before they turn the Jeep toward home.
